methodsAttempted.query: site:ucr.edu University of California Riverside generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Provost/XCITE generative AI guidelines, an Inside UCR student announcement, XCITE classroom guidance, and the Provost document landing page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucr.edu UCR generative AI approved tools privacy data security ChatGPT Google Gemini
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official ITS Generative AI at UCR page, a later Inside UCR Google AI tools story, career guidance, and low-specificity job-posting material.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucr.edu UCR AI academic integrity ChatGPT syllabus students allowed prohibited
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found general Student Conduct academic-integrity policy, an official UCR News interview, and course-specific Computer Science syllabus results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucr.edu UCR artificial intelligence research guidance IRB generative AI privacy human subjects
methodsAttempted.notes: Search returned research papers and symposium PDFs rather than official generative-AI research policy or IRB guidance.

Verified UCR-controlled hosts used in this bundle: provost.ucr.edu, teaching.ucr.edu, its.ucr.edu, insideucr.ucr.edu, and conduct.ucr.edu. Robots.txt disallowed administrative and search paths but did not disallow the fetched public evidence URLs.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located UCR Provost/XCITE instructional generative AI guidelines, XCITE classroom guidance, ITS tool and data-handling guidance, an Inside UCR student-facing announcement, and general Student Conduct academic-integrity policy. Claims are scoped to those sources. UCR News commentary, a course syllabus, career-center guidance, a job posting, and unrelated research PDFs were rejected or limited because they do not establish central university policy.

artifacts.title: University of California, Riverside Generative AI Policy Evidence Review
artifacts.summary: This staged review found official UCR instructional generative-AI guidelines, XCITE teaching guidance, ITS supported-tool and data-privacy guidance, a student-facing Inside UCR announcement, and general academic-integrity definitions. UCR evidence reviewed here emphasizes instructor/course-level expectations, responsible use, citation and accountability, and public-data limits for AI tools that have not passed campus security review. Course-specific syllabi, UCR News commentary, career advice, job-posting text, and research PDFs were rejected or limited as policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No public formal university-wide rule allowing or prohibiting all student generative-AI use was found in this run; extracted claims are limited to the official sources fetched.
The general academic-integrity source does not mention generative AI and should be used only as background for UCR misconduct definitions.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
